feed.title: Farenda
feed.subtitle: Yet another programming blog. Articles around Java, Clojure, Python, and other things around IT.

entry.title: Caesar cipher in Java
entry.summary: One of the simplest cryptographic algorithms is Caesar cipher. Maybe it’s not really secure, but shows how the synchronous ciphers work. Intro The cipher method is named after Julius Caesar, who presumably have used it to encrypt his military messages (see the history section at Wikipedia). Maybe at that time it was the state of the art. Anyway, the algorithm is good to know in case you hap...

entry.title: Towers of Hanoi recursive version
entry.summary: Towers of Hanoi is a well known puzzle, very often used to teach recursion. In this post we show how to solve the puzzle recursively. The rules There are three towers/pegs/rods with a number of disks on one of them. The disks are of different sizes, stacked one on another, and the sorted from smallest to the biggest (on the bottom). The objective is to move entire stack to another peg, but:...

entry.title: Top 10 sites to practice programming
entry.summary: HackerRank HackerRank is currently my favourite programming site. There is a lot of exercises and they are split in different categories - Algorithms, Java, Functional programming, Databases, etc. Everyone will find something interesting. In many cases solutions can be submitted using different languages - solve one in Java and then try the same in a functional language! I rea...

entry.title: Spring component scan exclude
entry.summary: How to exclude classes/packages from Component Scan in Spring Framework? The task is a bit tricky, especially when regexp is involved. See how it works! Example Spring beans Let’s say that we’ve got the following two classes in com.farenda.java.spring.excludescan package: package com.farenda.java.spring.excludescan; import org.springframework.stereotype.Service; @Service public class Exc...

entry.title: Spring Boot "Hello, World"
entry.summary: Problem: How to create “Hello, World” app in Spring Boot? Solution: Gradle build script will all required dependencies: buildscript { repositories { jcenter() maven { url "http://repo.spring.io/snapshot" } maven { url "http://repo.spring.io/milestone" } } dependencies { // Provides tasks to create executable JAR and run project from source. ...
